title: Use errors.Is for comparing ErrOCIRuntime errors
body:
Use errors.Is for comparing ErrOCIRuntime errors

This simplifies checking for ErrOCIRuntime type of errors by using go's
built-in `errors.Is` function.

In order to make this practical, some errors now wrap `ErrOCIRuntime`.
Namely: `ErrOCIRuntimePermissionDenied` and `ErrOCIRuntimeNotFound`.

Given that some errors might now wrap `ErrOCIRuntime`, the error
formatting in the podman command's output was adjusted to take this into
account.

I am a little concerned about doing this piecemail.  IE Should we switch from using errors.Cause-> errors.Is and all other uses of the "errors" modules.

This is going to be a far bigger change and needs to be discussed, I believe.  Definitely should not be done until post 3.0 release.

ah... seems that I gotta fix some tests before this works.

> I am a little concerned about doing this piecemail. IE Should we switch from using errors.Cause-> errors.Is and all other uses of the "errors" modules.
> 
> This is going to be a far bigger change and needs to be discussed, I believe. Definitely should not be done until post 3.0 release.

Do you think this change is too disruptive? I can abandon this if it would be too much of a hassle for the code-base. I was just trying to do some clean-up. But if the cleanup dirties the code-base more than it really cleans up, then it's no use. If folks think it's worth to start moving away from the custom errors module and into golang's error functions, that's fine and I could try to help. It's fine if this waits post 3.0, it's not urgent.
No I think it is time we started looking at this, but I want limited risk since we are hoping to release end of the week.

> No I think it is time we started looking at this, but I want limited risk since we are hoping to release end of the week.

sounds good. Let's then continue this work until after that.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
